home.description: I love the universe of web programming, design and web development, and general technology.

about.title: About
about.intro: Hello! I'm Israel Moreno, a Full Stack web developer based in Spain.
about.description: Full Stack Web Developer with over 3 years of experience leading the creation of high-performance platforms and digital products. Specialized in agile development with modern stacks (React, Next.js and Supabase) and robust architectures in Laravel (PHP).
about.mission: My current approach integrates Artificial Intelligence tools (AI-driven development) to maximize delivery speed, optimize code cleanliness, and ensure extreme performance (100% Core Web Vitals). Expert in payment gateways, complex API integrations (including OpenAI), and process automation.

items.skills: Web development, JSON, and modern web technologies

items.school: National University of Distance Education - U.N.E.D.

responsibilities: Software Engineering: Technical leadership and development of scalable, high-availability web platforms using Laravel, React and Vue.js.
Optimization & AI: Advanced integration of OpenAI models to automate internal workflows and personalize the user experience.
Data Architecture & Security: Design and implementation of complex relational databases and secure payment gateway configuration for global transactions.
Frontend Performance: Development of dynamic, ultra-fast interfaces, optimizing component architecture to meet the most demanding performance standards.
Development and maintenance of web applications using PHP, JavaScript and MySQL.
Web layout using HTML, CSS, Bootstrap.
Handling and optimization of XML and JSON data formats.

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: Combining professional experience with personal projects
projects.otherProjectsDescription: More work done during my professional career

list.title: Restaurant Platform & Booking System
list.description: Full stack web platform for PopStars Madrid (gastronomy, music and live show), built with React and Supabase. It includes a booking system with availability validation, a dynamic menu, an automated waitlist, and a custom admin panel.
list.long_description: PopStars Madrid is the new trendy restaurant in Madrid, where gastronomy merges with live music and entertainment. I independently developed its complete web platform as a React + Vite SPA (with Rolldown, the new Rust-based bundler), using Supabase as a serverless backend (PostgreSQL, Auth, Realtime and Edge Functions) to orchestrate every business flow: table reservations, corporate events, tardeo, and menu management.
list.long_description_2: I made the architecture decisions end to end. Critical business logic lives on the server through Supabase Edge Functions, isolated from the client, and I prioritized performance (aggressive code splitting with one chunk per page and anticipatory route preloading) alongside a distinctive visual experience: a global Three.js layer mounted as a fixed, persistent element that renders continuously over the entire SPA without being destroyed between navigations, integrating its lifecycle with React Router.
list.long_description_3: Beyond the public site, I built a private admin panel with its own authentication and real business automations. The platform includes:
details: Server-side booking logic: the flow is handled in a Supabase Edge Function (public-create-reservation) that evaluates availability and either confirms the reservation instantly or offers to join the waitlist. The calendar, built from scratch in React, only enables Fridays, Saturdays and Sundays and validates closed dates synced with Supabase.

Robust client-side validation: checks for available day, complete fields, email format, international phone prefix, mandatory notes for groups of +10 people, and privacy acceptance, with specific per-error messages via SweetAlert2.

Waitlist with serverless auto-promotion: when a spot opens up, the server-side logic (Edge Function / PostgreSQL trigger) automatically notifies the first person on the list, with no polling or cron in the frontend.

Single-use QR loyalty system: after tardeo signup (Edge Function public-tardeo-signup) a unique token is generated per user; the QR is rendered client-side and redeemed on-site with a camera scanner (native BarcodeDetector + html5-qrcode fallback) that marks the token as used and detects already-redeemed ones.
Private admin panel (/dashboard) with its own authentication: management of all reservations and restaurant shifts, the QR redemption scanner, and resending the voucher by email.
WhatsApp attendance confirmation: the customer confirms their reservation with a single tap from a button.
Dynamic menu from Supabase: backend-managed menu with prices, descriptions and allergen tags, navigable through a category carousel (Swiper).
Distinctive visual experience: a persistent 3D layer with Three.js and an adaptive hero video based on device orientation (vertical on mobile, horizontal on desktop).
Marketing and performance: integrated conversion tracking (Google Tag Manager with an event on confirmed reservation, Meta Pixel and TikTok Pixel) and full SEO/Open Graph for a hospitality business.

list.title: Multisite CMS (TALL Stack)
list.description: Custom multisite platform built with Laravel and the TALL Stack (Tailwind, Alpine.js, Livewire) that manages multiple musical shows from a single codebase, each with its own visual identity, content and analytics. Cinematic scroll animations with GSAP and AVIF-optimized media.
list.long_description: The beon. Entertainment project consisted of designing and developing a multisite web platform for a theatrical producer with multiple musicals running at once. The challenge was making each show work as an independent site —with its own visual identity, URL, language and content— while keeping a single, maintainable and scalable codebase. It is built on the TALL Stack (Tailwind CSS, Alpine.js, Livewire and Laravel).
list.long_description_2: As a developer at beon. Worldwide, I played a central role in its conception, development and delivery, effectively managing both time and resources. It remains one of my most significant contributions to date: an architecture that allows launching a new show without duplicating components, reusing the base and applying a complete visual theme per show.
list.long_description_3: These are the platform's technical highlights:
details: Multisite architecture: each show is an independent site (its own visual identity, content, language and analytics) served from a single Laravel codebase, allowing the platform to scale to new productions without rewriting components.
Multi-theme theming with Tailwind: color palette and design tokens defined per show, generating a complete visual theme per site at build time without duplicating the interface.

Reactive hybrid architecture (TALL Stack): Livewire provides server-side reactivity (interaction tracking, validated forms) and Alpine.js the client-side interactivity (menus, slide-over panels, gallery zoom that follows the cursor), all with no full page reloads.

Cinematic scroll animation: a hero section using GSAP + ScrollTrigger and sticky positioning to pin the images while the text advances with scroll, plus an animated particle canvas in pure JavaScript.

Responsive AVIF image pipeline: 140+ picture elements with per-breakpoint variants (mobile/desktop) served from a CDN, with automatic conversion and optimization for high performance despite the heavy media load.
Internationalization (i18n): per-language localized routes (/es, /en) with translated, SEO-friendly semantic slugs for each show.
Independent analytics per production: each site integrates its own measurement stack and cookie consent (GDPR), enabling isolated performance tracking for every musical.
Forms with server-side anti-spam protection (honeypot and server-side validation) and Laravel CSRF protection.
Technical SEO: JSON-LD structured data (schema.org) per production, Open Graph and Twitter Cards for optimal social sharing.

list.title: Ticketing & E-commerce Platform
list.description: Web platform for CTx, the largest tech event in Southern Europe (+50k attendees, +150 speakers). Its core is a custom ticketing and e-commerce system built from scratch (TALL Stack) with integration of the Spanish bank gateway Redsys (card and Bizum), invoicing, multi-platform tracking and on-demand gallery downloads.
list.long_description: CTx Tech Experience Hub is the largest tech event in Southern Europe, held in Seville. As a developer at beon. Worldwide, I participated in building this platform, whose core is a complete custom ticketing and e-commerce system built with the TALL Stack (Laravel, Livewire, Alpine.js and Tailwind), with no reliance on any third-party ticketing platform.
list.long_description_2: The highest technical challenge was the Redsys payment integration, Spain's standard bank gateway. I implemented the full flow: HMAC-SHA256 signing of the parameters, a POST redirect to the bank's terminal (supporting card and Bizum), response signature validation, handling of the bank's asynchronous notification, and order state management (pending, paid, failed) with idempotency to avoid processing a payment twice.
list.long_description_3: Around that core I built the rest of the platform's pieces:
details: Two-step checkout with Livewire: a catalog of 7 ticket types (from free to corporate) filterable by attendee profile, a real-time cart, promo codes and server-side VAT (21%) calculation.
Payments with Redsys (card and Bizum): HMAC-SHA256 signature, POST redirect to the bank terminal, response validation, asynchronous notification and order state management with idempotency.
Fiscally correct invoicing: distinction between a simplified receipt and a full invoice with tax ID, with separate email flows and PDF templates after payment.
Admin panel: order management by status, invoice download, attendee management by ticket type and QR access validation.
Multi-platform e-commerce tracking: purchase-funnel events (enhanced e-commerce) sent simultaneously to Google Analytics 4, GTM, Meta, TikTok and LinkedIn to measure cross-channel conversions.
On-demand ZIP gallery download: hundreds of lazy-loaded images, filterable by day and room, with the ZIP generated on the server on the fly (no pre-generated files).

Advanced animations: a generative WebGL background (lightning effect), GSAP + ScrollTrigger scroll animations, letter-by-letter word rotation with Alpine.js, and a partners carousel with auto-scroll and hover pause.
Large-scale dynamic sections: management of +150 speakers with detailed profiles and a directory of +1000 startups and participating companies.
Integration with Aurora, the event's AI-powered networking platform, and full ES/EN i18n with localized slugs (/es/entradas, /en/tickets).

list.title: Retail Fair Website
list.description: Development of the web platform for ZigZag, the first retail expansion fair in the Iberian Peninsula, held at the Palacio de Convenciones in Madrid.
list.long_description: ZigZag Expo Retail is a specialized event where brands, franchises, shopping centers, real estate developers, and retail sector consultants come together to boost their growth, find new locations, and generate strategic alliances. As a Web Developer at beon. Worldwide, I participated in creating this modern and attractive web platform.
details: Development of a corporate website with modern design and fluid animations to capture visitors' attention.
Integrated ticket sales system with different access types (Premium, General) and early bird pricing.
Multilingual content management in 5 languages (Spanish, English, Italian, Portuguese, and French) to reach an international audience.
Dynamic sections to showcase exhibitors, event categories (Fashion, Food, Home, Sports, etc.) and statistics.
Integration of information about the ZigZag Party event at Teatro Calderón with featured speakers.
Performance optimization with AVIF format images and fully responsive design.

list.title: Ciclo Healthy - Fitness Event Website
list.description: Complete development of the web platform for Ciclo Healthy, a free fitness activities event at Plaza Río 2's lookout terrace, with registration system and admin panel.
list.long_description: Ciclo Healthy is an event of free healthy sessions held at the Mirador Terrace of Plaza Río 2 in Madrid. The event includes activities such as Barré, Running, and Yoga taught by professional instructors. This was my first solo project at beon. Worldwide, where I autonomously managed the entire development cycle from planning to delivery.
details: Complete development of a corporate website for event promotion with modern and attractive design.
Integrated user registration system for signing up for different activities (Barré, Running, Yoga).
Admin panel (Dashboard) for managing registrations and tracking sign-ups.
Dynamic sections with information about instructors, event dates, and types of activities.
First project developed independently at beon. Worldwide, managing timelines and deliverables autonomously.
Responsive design optimized for all devices with modern format images.

list.title: Event Landing Page
list.description: Development of an optimized landing page for the 'Finca Alfoliz Origen' event, with an integrated system for ticket management and sales.
list.long_description: Part of my work as a Web Developer at beon. Worldwide, this project focused on creating a high-performance landing page to promote the event and efficiently manage ticket sales. The priority was resource optimization, ensuring a fast and smooth user experience, from information consultation to the final ticket purchase.
details: Design and development of a landing page focused on conversion and clarity of event information.
Direct integration with a ticketing platform for simplified and secure ticket management.
Image optimization and load times to ensure maximum performance even on mobile devices.
Payment gateway configuration to securely process ticket purchase transactions.
Administration panel for tracking sales and managing event attendees.
